# Evaluating AGI: BenchPress Scaffold


<!-- WARNING: THIS FILE WAS AUTOGENERATED! DO NOT EDIT! -->

A scaffold to test new AGI benchmarks using the `benchpress` matrix
completion method.

When proposing a new benchmark (especially in competitions), it’s hard
to know if it’s actually measuring something **novel**, or just
redundant with existing tests (like AIME or MMLU). This scaffold uses
the
[BenchPress](https://open.substack.com/pub/dimitrisp/p/you-dont-need-to-run-every-eval)
methodology to predict how models *should* score based on the entire
ecosystem of existing benchmarks.

- If your new benchmark’s scores can be perfectly predicted, it’s highly
  correlated with existing tests (redundant).
- If they can’t be predicted, you’ve found a novel axis of evaluation
  (or it’s just noise).

This toolkit provides a quick, mathematical way to check and defend the
novelty of your benchmarks.

## Quickstart Tutorial

For a hands-on introduction you can run directly in Colab or Kaggle,
check out the **[Tutorial
Notebook](https://github.com/kafkasl/evaluating-agi/blob/main/tutorial.ipynb)**.

## Installation

You can install the package directly from GitHub to use the evaluation
scaffold anywhere (Kaggle, Colab, Local):

``` sh
pip install git+https://github.com/kafkasl/evaluating-agi.git
```

## Usage

The core of the library is the `check_novelty` function. It calculates a
**Leave-One-Out (LOO) median error** to test if your benchmark is novel.

- `~3 pts median error`: **Redundant** (Highly predictable from other
  benchmarks)
- `~8 pts median error`: **Somewhat Novel**
- `~12+ pts median error`: **Novel** (Measures something genuinely new)
- `~18+ pts median error`: **Noise** (Random or broken benchmark)

``` python
from evaluating_agi.benchpress import check_novelty

# 1. Provide the scores your benchmark assigned to various models.
#    (Keys must match the valid model IDs in the BenchPress matrix)
my_benchmark_scores = {
    'gpt-4.5': 85.0,
    'claude-3.7-sonnet': 82.5,
    'o3-mini-high': 88.0,
    'claude-opus-4': 78.0
}

# 2. Evaluate how novel these scores are against the existing AGI landscape
report = check_novelty(my_benchmark_scores, name='LogicPuzzle-100')
```

      Model                        Actual   Pred   Err
      Claude Opus 4                  78.0   85.3   7.3
      o3-mini (high)                 88.0   81.4   6.6
      Claude 3.7 Sonnet              82.5   85.6   3.1
      GPT-4.5                        85.0   83.4   1.6
      Median LOO: 4.8 pts → redundant
